annotations.label: Quasigroup (informal)

anchor.snippet: a quasigroup is an algebraic structure that resembles a group

annotations.note: No Quasigroup typeclass or structure appears anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonempty associative quasigroup is a group

anchor.snippet: a nonempty associative quasigroup is a group

annotations.note: No quasigroup structure exists in Mathlib, so this proposition is not stated.

annotations.label: Loop

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup that has an identity element is called a loop

annotations.note: Mathlib has no algebraic Loop (nonassociative) structure.

annotations.label: Quasigroup (algebra, Latin square property)

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup ( Q , ∗) is a set Q with a binary operation ∗

annotations.note: No quasigroup definition found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left and right division

anchor.snippet: The operations ' \ ' and ' / ' are called, respectively, left division and right division

annotations.note: Mathlib has Div/division on groups but not the quasigroup left/right division operators.

annotations.label: Empty quasigroup

anchor.snippet: The empty set equipped with the empty binary operation satisfies this definition of a quasigroup

annotations.note: No quasigroup structure exists to instantiate on Empty.

annotations.label: Variety

anchor.snippet: Algebraic structures that satisfy axioms that are given solely by identities are called varieties

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general notion of a variety in universal algebra.

annotations.label: Right-quasigroup

anchor.snippet: A right-quasigroup ( Q , ∗, /) is a type (2, 2) algebra that satisfies the identities

annotations.note: No right-quasigroup typeclass present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left-quasigroup

anchor.snippet: A left-quasigroup ( Q , ∗, \) is a type (2, 2) algebra that satisfies the identities

annotations.note: No left-quasigroup typeclass present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quasigroup (universal algebra)

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup ( Q , ∗, \, /) is a type (2, 2, 2) algebra

annotations.note: No (2,2,2) quasigroup structure in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence of quasigroup definitions

anchor.snippet: Hence if ( Q , ∗) is a quasigroup according to the definition of the previous section

annotations.note: Without a quasigroup definition this equivalence is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Loop (with identity)

anchor.snippet: A loop is a quasigroup with an identity element

annotations.note: Mathlib has no algebraic loop typeclass.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of identity and inverses in a loop

anchor.snippet: It follows that the identity element, e , is unique

annotations.note: Identity uniqueness holds for monoids in Mathlib but the loop-specific result is absent.

annotations.label: Pique (pointed idempotent quasigroup)

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup with an idempotent element is called a pique

annotations.note: No pique definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associative loop is a group

anchor.snippet: A loop that is associative is a group

annotations.note: No loop structure in Mathlib, so this proposition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bol loop

anchor.snippet: a Bol loop is a loop that satisfies either

annotations.note: No Bol loop definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Moufang loop

anchor.snippet: A loop that is both a left and right Bol loop is a Moufang loop

annotations.note: Grep for `Moufang` in Mathlib returns no hits.

annotations.label: Semisymmetric quasigroup

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup is semisymmetric if any of the following equivalent identities hold

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Induced semisymmetric quasigroup on Q^3

anchor.snippet: every quasigroup Q induces a semisymmetric quasigroup

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Totally symmetric quasigroup

anchor.snippet: a totally symmetric quasigroup (sometimes abbreviated TS-quasigroup ) in which all conjugates coincide

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Steiner quasigroup (squag) and sloop

anchor.snippet: Idempotent total symmetric quasigroups are precisely (i.e. in a bijection with) Steiner triples

annotations.note: No Steiner quasigroup or Steiner triple system in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weakly totally anti-symmetric quasigroup

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup ( Q , ∗) is called weakly totally anti-symmetric

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Totally anti-symmetric quasigroup

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup ( Q , ∗) is called totally anti-symmetric

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group is a loop

anchor.snippet: Every group is a loop

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Group` but no `Loop` typeclass to instantiate from it.

annotations.label: Integers with subtraction

anchor.snippet: The integers Z (or the rationals Q or the reals R ) with subtraction

annotations.note: `Int.instSub` exists but is not packaged as a quasigroup structure.

annotations.label: Nonzero rationals with division

anchor.snippet: The nonzero rationals Q × (or the nonzero reals R × ) with division

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Rat`/`Real` division but no quasigroup instance for it.

annotations.label: Vector space midpoint quasigroup

anchor.snippet: Any vector space over a field of characteristic not equal to 2 forms an idempotent

annotations.note: The midpoint operation isn't packaged as a quasigroup in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Steiner triple system quasigroup

anchor.snippet: Every Steiner triple system defines an idempotent , commutative quasigroup

annotations.note: Neither Steiner triple systems nor quasigroups exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonassociative loop of order 8 (hyperbolic quaternion units)

anchor.snippet: The set {±1, ±i, ±j, ±k} where ii = jj = kk = +1

annotations.note: Hyperbolic quaternions and the resulting loop are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonzero octonions form a Moufang loop

anchor.snippet: The nonzero octonions form a nonassociative loop under multiplication

annotations.note: Grep for `Octonion` in Mathlib returns no hits.

annotations.label: Associative quasigroup is empty or a group

anchor.snippet: An associative quasigroup is either empty or is a group

annotations.note: No quasigroup definition in Mathlib to state this against.

annotations.label: Zassenhaus commutative Moufang loop

anchor.snippet: The following construction is due to Hans Zassenhaus

annotations.note: No Moufang loop infrastructure exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nonzero elements of a division algebra

anchor.snippet: the nonzero elements of any division algebra form a quasigroup

annotations.note: Mathlib has `DivisionRing` but does not state the quasigroup structure on its units.

annotations.label: Cancellation property of quasigroups

anchor.snippet: Quasigroups have the cancellation property

annotations.note: Mathlib has `mul_left_cancel`/`mul_right_cancel` for groups/cancel monoids, but not for a quasigroup typeclass.

annotations.label: Latin square property determines third variable

anchor.snippet: The Latin square property of quasigroups implies that, given any two of the three variables

annotations.note: Not formalized; no quasigroup or Latin square infrastructure.

annotations.label: Left and right multiplication operators

anchor.snippet: the left and right multiplication operators L x , R x : Q → Q

annotations.note: Mathlib has `mulLeft`/`mulRight` for groups but not as quasigroup-defining operators.

annotations.label: Magma is quasigroup iff multiplication operators are bijective

anchor.snippet: A magma Q is a quasigroup precisely when all these operators

annotations.note: Not present; no quasigroup definition to characterize.

annotations.label: Multiplication table of finite quasigroup is a Latin square

anchor.snippet: The multiplication table of a finite quasigroup is a Latin square

annotations.note: Neither quasigroups nor Latin squares are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Latin squares give quasigroup multiplication tables

anchor.snippet: every Latin square can be taken as the multiplication table of a quasigroup in many ways

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Latin square property for infinite quasigroups

anchor.snippet: the Latin square property says that each row and each column of the infinite array will contain every possible value precisely once

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invertibility of quasigroup binary operation

anchor.snippet: The binary operation of a quasigroup is invertible

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left and right inverse in a loop

anchor.snippet: Every loop element has a unique left and right inverse

annotations.note: No loop structure exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two-sided inverses in a loop

anchor.snippet: A loop is said to have ( two-sided ) inverses

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inverse property loops (LIP, RIP, AAIP, WIP)

anchor.snippet: There are some stronger notions of inverses in loops that are often useful

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inverse property loop

anchor.snippet: A loop has the inverse property if it has both the left and right inverse properties

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: One-sided inverse properties imply two-sided inverses

anchor.snippet: Any loop that satisfies the left, right, or antiautomorphic inverse properties automatically has two-sided inverses

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quasigroup/loop homomorphism

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup or loop homomorphism is a map

annotations.note: Mathlib has `MonoidHom`/`MulHom` but no quasigroup homomorphism.

annotations.label: Quasigroup homotopy

anchor.snippet: A quasigroup homotopy from Q to P is a triple

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isotopy and isotopic quasigroups

anchor.snippet: An isotopy is a homotopy for which each of the three maps

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Autotopy

anchor.snippet: An autotopy is an isotopy from a quasigroup to itself

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every quasigroup is isotopic to a loop

anchor.snippet: Every quasigroup is isotopic to a loop

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bruck–Toyoda theorem

anchor.snippet: Every medial quasigroup is isotopic to an abelian group by the Bruck–Toyoda theorem

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugates / parastrophes

anchor.snippet: That makes a total of six quasigroup operations, which are called the conjugates or parastrophes

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isostrophic operations (paratopy)

anchor.snippet: the operations are said to be isostrophic to each other

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-ary quasigroup

anchor.snippet: An n - ary quasigroup is a set with an n -ary operation

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nullary, unary, binary quasigroups

anchor.snippet: A 0-ary, or nullary , quasigroup is just a constant element of Q

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Iterated group operation as multiary quasigroup

anchor.snippet: An example of a multiary quasigroup is an iterated group operation

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Irreducible n-ary quasigroup

anchor.snippet: An n -ary quasigroup is irreducible if its operation cannot be factored

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-ary group

anchor.snippet: An n -ary quasigroup with an n -ary version of associativity is called an n -ary group

annotations.note: Not present in Mathlib.
